auth.loginSubtitle: Use your account credentials to continue.

hero.subtitle: A production-grade Turborepo starter with three wired apps, shared UI, a typed API client, auth, i18n and tests — so day one feels like day one hundred.
hero.note: MIT licensed · no lock-in · clone and go

features.title: The parts you rewrite on every project
features.subtitle: Not another hello-world. This is the glue that actually talks to a backend.

items.title: Three apps, one system
items.desc: Landing, user app and admin panel share the same UI package, tokens and dark mode. Drop one you don't need with a single command.

items.title: Same-origin API proxy
items.desc: The browser hits /api/*, Next forwards to your backend. The backend URL never reaches the bundle and CORS becomes a non-problem.

items.title: Cookie auth, end to end
items.desc: Better Auth works the same in the browser, server components, route handlers and middleware. No JWT plumbing, no refresh dance.

items.title: Typed API client
items.desc: Point Orval at your OpenAPI spec for typed React Query hooks — or write them by hand. Both modes live side by side.

items.title: i18n that survives SSR
items.desc: next-intl with locale route segments, Vietnamese and English out of the box, and a switcher that keeps your place.

items.title: Real tests, real CI
items.desc: Vitest and Testing Library for units, Playwright across three browsers for E2E — all gated in GitHub Actions.

showcase.title: Strict where it counts, quiet where it doesn't
points: Husky + lint-staged + commitlint on every commit
Knip and Syncpack keep the graph honest

pricing.title: Free to clone. Yours to ship.
pricing.subtitle: It's a boilerplate — the tiers below are wired and waiting for your billing provider.

items.a: Yes. Strict types, security headers, tests and CI are wired from the first commit. You add product code, not plumbing.

items.q: Can I remove an app I don't need?
items.a: Run pnpm remove-app <name>. It deletes the app and cleans every reference — knip, changeset and docker-compose.
Any. All client traffic is proxied through Next to BACKEND_URL, so bring REST or GraphQL — the browser never sees it.

items.q: Do I have to use the API codegen?
items.a: No. Orval is optional. Write React Query hooks by hand or generate them from a spec — both coexist.

cta.title: Your next project starts at day one hundred.
cta.subtitle: Clone the repo, point it at your backend, and ship the thing that actually matters.

footer.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Turborepo and stubbornness.

meta.title: turbo/starter — Production Next.js monorepo boilerplate
meta.description: A production-grade Turborepo starter with three wired apps, shared UI, a typed API client, auth, i18n and tests. Clone it and ship.
